free_text: Reaction rate for carbon burning in massive stars
USIAS, Strasbourg
Data contained in and taken from Supplemental Material
http://link.aps.org/supplemental/
The experiment was performed at the
ATLAS accelerator at Argonne National Laboratory
using Gammasphere in coincidence with silicon detectors
Isotopically enriched 12C targets
with thickness of about 30-50 mu-g/cm2 were used.
Gamma rays were detected using Gammasphere,
which is an array of about 100 Compton-suppressed Ge
spectrometers.
Charged particle were detected using compact
array of three annular double-sided silicon strip
detectors located inside the target chamber. Each Si
detector had a thickness of 500 mu-m and was
subdivided into 16 rings and 16 wedges covering the
angular ranges of 147-170deg, 123-143deg and 17-32deg,
respectively. The total solid angle coverage was
about 25% of 4PI.
Coincidence between gamma array and Si array
was required.
Source of uncertainties not described
Compiled by S.H.
OS. Data correction in sub.2
Total cross sections were obtained as a sum of partial
cross section SIG(p1),SIG(p2),...,SIG(a1),..., from
12C(12C,p)23Na and 12C(12C,a)20Ne reactions using
previously measured  averaged cross section ratios
SIG(tot)/SIG(pi),SIG(tot)/SIG(ai) etc.from charged
particle experiments by Becker et al. and
Mazarakis et al.
